title: X-tra Creamy Argentinian Chicken Empanadas
description: Kid-friendly Argentinian dish featuring plum jam, with a winey and creamy twist.
instructions: In a medium bowl, mix together the chicken, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together the plum jam, gruyère cheese, and chopped red onion.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the plum jam mixture onto one half of each dough circle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Add a few pieces of the cooked chicken on top of the plum jam mixture.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten eggs and sprinkle with chopped cilantro if using.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with the melted butter.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
